item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Been meaning to find this one again! It's stuck with me since I read it. I already upvoted it back then tho

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as no source found indicates it has been released under Creative Commons. As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dear Gracious Commenter,

You made me realize that there will come a day when we can +1 people's comments and replies. Thank you for that.

Yours truly,
-The AWCY? Re-Uprising

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't care one way or another about the SCP, but what's the idea with terminating or D-classing those affected?

I don't see how being selectively deaf should be worth killing.

As an aside, it's the newbie in me speaking, but I kinda want to know what happens if you exposed those affected to Music SCPs. I bet there's at least a couple in the mainlist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP is evil incarnate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure what vote to give this yet. I love the concept but I'd love to see something of a stronger grounding in its purpose, particularly with the AWCY tie, which at the moment looks a bit like an afterthought. But props for it being SCP-14'33".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not really into music, but that was excellent explanation of idea behind this SCP. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for Simon and Garfunkel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just imagine, all you'll ever be able to listen to afterwards is noisecore…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the scariest SCP on the entire website.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reading lots of weird shit

"Are we cool yet?"

FUCK YOU.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my God, that's absolutely awful!

The lyrics, the delivery, the artist photo, the artist name… wow

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why did I not read this before… and I would personally like to hunt down AECY for ruining the music.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Concise, imaginative, well-written + the addendum made me smile. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Ossify Wall Street," anyone?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, I'm not crazy about the one AWCY line but the one random BNL line balanced it out. Besides, I caught myself hesitating while looking up the real version of 4'33" and I would call that a successful creepypasta there. :p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Worse, it's "Fuck you up + anomalous + art = Are we cool yet?". Anything which can be AWCY must be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this object. I like AWCY? I don't like that this is an AWCY object, or is, at least, heavily implied to be. This, along with a few objects that have since been deleted or had their emphasis shifted, shows the problem that I have with AWCY, which is basically "Fuck you up + anomalous + Are we cool yet? = art." In my headcanon, there are numerous factions of the anomalous art movement, including AWCY, and there are many more factions of AWCY. The fact that one of them might actually think this doesn't make it any less irritating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really loved this one. The AWCY? reference did it for me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After some Googling, I actually found a song called "Are We Cool Yet?"

Appropriately enough for the purposes of this article, it's a rap song.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
all the other statements are, AFAIK, from song lyrics.

I kind of hate that this isn't noted anywhere in the article. It is both something the Foundation would notice and something it would consider worth mentioning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What if TL333s is right? What if "Are we cool yet?" is from a song too? From a song that AWCY? members wrote. The song that does something anomalous. And the Foundation doesn't even know it exists yet.

And 1433 was actually created by benign aliens to save people from Music Plague inadvertently caused by AWCY? and in the end only those exposed to 1433 will survive.

Alright, maybe not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THIS.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "The Sound of Silence", "God", "My My Hey Hey", "Eclipse", "American Pie", and "It's All Been Done", to be specific.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually believe that AWCY the group has nothing to do with this at all. It's just saying their name because.

Normally, I would agree with you there, but all the other statements are, AFAIK, from song lyrics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that is a better name

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is why I love the Foundation - only on this website would the phrase "the antishark has no anomalous panic-causing properties" make sense in any context.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I should point out, the antishark doesn't have any anomalous panic-causing properties. The panic during which five people were killed was the completely normal and understandable result of an invisible, intangible shark killing people gorily in a crowded indoor swimming pool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually believe that AWCY the group has nothing to do with this at all. It's just saying their name because.

I do sort of question that final note. I'm having a knee-jerk "DAT'S RACIST" reaction, but that may just be me. Granted, you could get the same effect with, say, death metal or sound collage and the right listener.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personally I think "The Day the Music Died" would be a better name for this SCP.

Also my headcanon says that the D-Class guy who could still hear rap music was a KKK member.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As music addict, this frightens me to the core. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I share your image of AWCY and their perspective-shifting emphasis. But I think that this fits with that theme perfectly. Consider the addendum test subject. He did not consider rap to be music, now he can either listen to rap or have no music at all. That seems like it is directly targeting people with a closed-minded and set definition of what art (music) is, a demographic that AWCY would defintely want to focus on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, most of the AWCY things *are* malicious… A piece of modern art that makes modern civilization LITERALLY crumble to dust and the people [DATA EXPUNGED] horribly… A shark that isn't actually there but still attacks people and causes people to be more freaked out than normal.. The painting that traumatizes people through the sheer horribleness of its images.. Those statues…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is well-executed but … I don't like it and I can't really articulate why. Maybe it's that in my head, AWCY uses anomalous art to naively "bust people out of their frame of reference, maaaan" and the damage they do is a side effect. This just seems malicious.

No vote, but don't take it personally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See, I upvoted, but I'm not sure the AWCY tie-in quite works here. But then, I've shown to not understand AWCY before (SCP-1057).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was awesome, then AWCY made an appearance and it was more awesomerer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is that the one where any music played will sound like rap to the listener?

Celine Dion suddenly became even more caustic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God dammit, now I have to go write that rap music SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a musician, the idea of being unable to hear music is creepy enough, but this whole SCP was *ahem* music to my ears. +1

Only one thing though - I think specifically naming the manufacturer of the tape as TDK would be more realistic than the 'of the type widely manufactured in the 1980s', but that may very well just be me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No problemo mi amigo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Argh, can't believe I missed that.

Thanks. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Replaced the SCP-XXXX, damn good work Smapti.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A quick one I came up with today, based on my favorite GoI.
